scope: AgenC first-class Write tool port, read-before-overwrite guard, permission flow, and registry surface.

requiredBehaviors: the tool is named Write
new files are created with the exact provided UTF-8 content
missing parent directories are created before writing
existing files are overwritten only after the write guards pass
create and overwrite successes return the source-compatible plain-text result strings
file mutation metadata reports create/write operations with additions and removals
edgeCases: plain-text error results are not JSON-wrapped envelopes
notebook files are rejected with a notebook-edit hint
content larger than the write cap is rejected before mutation

requiredBehaviors: existing files require a prior session read before Write may overwrite them
unread existing files return the source-compatible read-first error message
a file changed since the recorded read returns the source-compatible modified-since-read message
full read snapshots compare raw disk content when rendered read content differs
a successful write records a fresh full snapshot so a follow-up write in the same session can proceed
edgeCases: direct headless tool invocations without a session id keep the established test harness bypass
live dispatch injects conversationId as __agencSessionId so session-scoped guards are active outside direct tests
external drift leaves the on-disk file unchanged after rejection

requiredBehaviors: Write requires approval by default
Write is registered as side-effecting for restart recovery
checkPermissions runs path validation before execution
deny rules produce deny decisions and outside-working-root paths produce ask decisions
permission checks classify the operation as write
edgeCases: active session plan files may be written through the dedicated plan-file carve-out
paths outside allowed roots are rejected before filesystem mutation
the registry marks Write as exclusive and not parallel-safe
commands: npm run test --workspace=@tetsuo-ai/runtime -- src/tools/system/file-write.test.ts src/permissions/path-validation.test.ts src/tool-registry.test.ts

requiredBehaviors: Write is registered in the first-class file tool group
Write remains visible by default to model-facing providers
the consolidated first-class file surface maps write to the Write tool name
the string argument field for Write is file_path
edgeCases: legacy compatibility filesystem tools remain separate from the first-class Write surface
Write is exported through runtime/src/tools/system/index.ts for consumers that construct tools directly
